events.title: I Swear
events.location: Mary Riepma Ross Media Arts Center-Van Brunt Visitors Center Room:Ross Theaters (123 & 124)
events.description: Showing at The Ross April 24-May 7. Visit theross.org for tickets, showtimes, and more information. Kirk Jones' emotionally engaging, funny and compelling film charts John Davidson's Tourette's diagnosis at the age of 14 years old. Set within 1980s Britain, the story follows him throughout his troubled teens and early adulthood, and explores this little known and entirely misunderstood condition, along with his attempts to live a 'normal' life against the odds.Ross MovieStatus: CONFIRMED | |

events.description: Day 2 of the Twenty-Fifth Annual Czech and Slovak Studies Workshop will consist of four sessions and will culminate in a special public event. Session I Borderlands of Memory: From Municipal Governance to Post-War Reckoning Location: BURN 301 Session II Nationalism and Ethics in Czech Music, Culture, and Visual Poetry Location: BURN 301 Session III Literary and Linguistic Frontiers Location: BURN 301 Session IV Between Heaven and Homeland: Religious Reform, Slavic Nationalism, and Bolshevik Utopianism Location: BURN 301 Special Public Event: Belousek Literary Feast Location: BURN 115 Alena Jirsek: Poems from a Concentration Camp by Josef apek Stephen Lahey: Krvav romn / Bloody Novel by Josef Vchal Jaroslav Ola: Miloslav (Miles) J. Breuer, the Czech-American Science Fiction WriterStatus: CONFIRMED | - |

events.title: Part 107 Remote Pilot Exam Prep Course
events.description: This 1-day (8-hour) course prepares students to pass the FAA Part 107 Aeronautical Knowledge Exam required to receive a remote pilot certificate (aka drone license). Covers aviation topics such as regulations, airspace, radio communication, weather, aircraft performance, and other basic aviation concepts necessary to pass the exam. Aviation experience is not essential to be successful in the course. This course is geared towards anyone interested in flying drones for commercial or public applications, including agriculture, spray drones, emergency management, law enforcement, real estate and land management, and photography. One-Day Drone Training from Nebraska ExtensionStatus: CONFIRMED | - |

events.title: Wellbeing Discussions
events.description: A supportive, low-intensity space for Raikes School students to explore a variety of wellbeing-related topics, pause, and reset. We engage in discussion for 20 to 30 minutes, followed by 10 to 15 minutes of guided meditation, and wrap up with 10 to 15 minutes of Q&A or additional discussion. Participants can direct what topics we spend time on. This is an open, drop-in group. Drop-In GroupStatus: CONFIRMED | - |

events.title: 2025-26 Methodology Applications Series
events.description: Pavel Chernyavskiy, assistant professor in the Department of Health Sciences at the University of Virginia, will lead the spring presentation of the MAP Academy's 2025-26 Methodology Applications Series. His presentation is titled, "Everything is Important and Everything is Correlated: Challenges and Opportunities of Working in Correlated Data." Much of the data in social and biomedical sciences features non-ignorable correlations that must be considered in analysis. This presentation will cover several projects rooted in education and public health that feature correlated data, the methodological hurdles and triumphs therein. It will also discuss some opportunities to extend existing models. The presentation is free and open to the public.Featuring Pavel Chernyavskiy, Ph.D.Status: CONFIRMED | - |

events.title: Motorcycle Trainings
events.description: The Nebraska Safety Center is offering Basic Rider Motorcycle courses in Lincoln, Nebraska. This two-day course provides riders with basic, introductory, formal instruction about how to safely operate a motorcycle. This course includes five hours of classroom instruction at the Nebraska Safety Center Classroom at NIC, along with ten hours of hands-on range instruction at Haleon. Participants who successfully complete the course will qualify for the motorcycle endorsement ("M") on their Operator's License. Motorcycles are provided for use during the course. The cost to participate is $275. For more information or to register, please visit: https://www.unk.edu/offices/safety_center/motorcycle.php.Status: CONFIRMED | - |

events.title: A Collection of Work by Scott McDonald, An Art Exhibition
events.description: This collection features watercolor and gouache works created over the past three years by Lincoln native artist Scott McDonald. The series explores themes of human existence, presenting a striking balance of beauty and symmetry within subject matter that can feel unsettling or uncomfortable. Through detailed depictions of bodies, hands, and skulls, Scott examines the inevitability of death - an aspect of life he believes should be embraced rather than feared. His work invites viewers to reconsider their perceptions, finding grace and harmony in themes often associated with discomfort. A self-taught watercolor artist, Scott applies techniques influenced by his earlier experience with acrylics, building texture and strong tonal contrasts within each piece. His artistic influences range from classical painters such as William-Adolphe Bouguereau and Caravaggio to surrealists Zdzisaw Beksinski and Salvador Dal, as well as contemporary artists including Jeremy Geddes and Michael Reedy. Scott has been employed at the University of Nebraska-Lincoln for 18 years. Over time, his artistic style has evolved from abstract and cartoon-inspired acrylic paintings to his current contemporary and dark surrealist watercolor and gouache series. His work has been displayed locally at Tugboat Gallery, Burkholder Gallery, and the Resonator Gallery. In 2022, he received the Best Newcomer award at the Lincoln Arts Festival and was featured as an emerging artist. Most recently, three of his watercolor pieces were included in Manifest Gallery's 22nd Season AQUACHROME Biennial exhibition. To learn more about Scott and his artwork, please visit scottmcdonaldartist.com. The NIC Art Gallery is located in the 2nd floor link at Nebraska Innovation Campus and is free and open to the public daily from 8:00 a.m. - 8:00 p.m. through April 26.Status: CONFIRMED | - |

events.title: 25th Annual Czech and Slovak Studies Workshop, Day 3
events.description: Day 3 of the Twenty-Fifth Annual Czech and Slovak Studies Workshop will consist of two sessions and will feature a museum visit as well as a film screening and discussion. Session V Antisemitism and Entangled Jewish Identities in the Czechoslovak Press and Exilic Diaspora Location: BURN 301 Session VI Narrating Trauma: Literature, Memorialization, and Seeking Justice Location: BURN 301 Participants will visit the Wilber Museum and conclude the Day 3 conference with a screening of The Art of Dissent at the ROSS, followed by a discussion with filmmaker James LeSuer, Alena Jirsek, Jacques Rupnik, and Veronika Tuckerova.Status: CONFIRMED | - |

After teaching the basics of kayaking and cleanup safety, we will spend the day paddling and picking up trash to give back to the beautiful Nebraska waterways. This is a grant-funded program, so transportation, boats, cleanup gear, lunch and instruction are all provided for a reduced cost to volunteers. $15 for students/members $45 for non-membersDay TripsStatus: CONFIRMED | - |

events.title: Tarot and the Art of Chance: A Workshop
events.description: Inspired by the exhibition "Hyphen American: Intersections of Identity" and its exploration of community and ritual, artist Toan Vuong leads "Tarot and the Art of Chance: Navigating Chaos and Meaning in a Visual World." This workshop offers practical guidance in reading tarot cards and considering how images acquire meaning through an artist's perspective. Participants will study the composition and symbolism of the traditional Rider-Waite-Smith deck, its historical development, and its connections to broader visual-art traditions. Registration is encouraged.Navigating Chaos and Meaning in a Visual WorldStatus: CONFIRMED | - |

events.title: 2026 CYFS Early Childhood Research Summit
events.description: The 2026 CYFS Early Childhood Research Summit aims to: • Communicate cutting-edge early childhood findings from NU-affiliated researchers and colleagues. • Link ongoing efforts in early childhood research, practice and policy. • Advance the science of early childhood education and development via connections among community partners, key stakeholders, policymakers, early childhood practitioners and researchers. The eighth biennial summit will consist of several presentations and related roundtable discussions on the research and practices that are improving the developmental trajectories of young children. The summit’s keynote speaker is Ying Xu, assistant professor of AI in the Department of Learning and Education and Harvard University. Her research focuses on designing AI technologies that promote language and literacy development, STEM learning and well-being for children and families.Status: CONFIRMED | - |

events.title: Cronos (4K Restoration)
events.location: Mary Riepma Ross Media Arts Center-Van Brunt Visitors Center Room:Ross Theater (Room 124)
events.description: ONE NIGHT ONLY ~ Tuesday, April 28 @ 7:30pm. Visit theross.org for tickets and more information. *4K Restoration* Guillermo del Toro made an auspicious and audacious feature debut with Cronos, a highly unorthodox tale about the seductiveness of the idea of immortality. Kindly antiques dealer Jess Gris (Federico Luppi) happens upon an ancient golden device in the shape of a scarab, and soon finds himself the possessor and victim of its sinister, addictive powers, as well as the target of a mysterious American named Angel (a delightfully crude and deranged Ron Perlman). Featuring marvelous makeup effects and the haunting imagery for which del Toro has become world-renowned, Cronos is a dark, visually rich, and emotionally captivating fantasy. TICKETS: $10 General Admission | $5 Friends Members & UNL Students.Ross EventStatus: CONFIRMED | |

events.title: Four Nights of a Dreamer (4K Restoration)
events.location: Mary Riepma Ross Media Arts Center-Van Brunt Visitors Center Room:Ross Theater (Room 124)
events.description: ONE NIGHT ONLY ~ Wednesday, April 29 @ 7:30 p.m. Visit theross.org for tickets, showtimes, and more information. The rarely screened Four Nights of a Dreamer is Robert Bresson's great forgotten masterpiece, a stark yet haunting ode to romantic idealism and the capriciousness of love. Adapted from Fyodor Dostoevsky's "White Nights," Four Nights follows Jacques (Guillaume des Forts), a lonely artist who roams bohemian Paris in search of the girl of his dreams. One night he saves a beautiful young woman, Marthe, from plunging into the Seine in despair over her rejection by an avoidant lover (Maurice Monnoyer). Jacques compassionately attempts to reunite Marthe with her beau, but his feelings for his new friend soon become less than platonic and his investment in her personal drama far from selfless. Four Nights of a Dreamer has been called the French master's "loveliest" work: with his signature minimalism, Bresson films the shimmering beauty of nocturnal Paris as it enfolds his characters in endless possibility-subtly capturing the wonder of unexpected connection and the mystery of fate. TICKETS: $10 General Admission | $5 Friends Members & UNL Students.Ross EventStatus: CONFIRMED | |

events.title: Health Equity Grand Rounds: Deadric Williams
events.description: Deadric Williams, PhD, will join us for our April Grand Rounds to discuss "Systemic Racism and the Three M's of Racial Inequality: Implications for Health Disparities." Dr. Williams is an Associate Professor of Sociology at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville. Increased attention has been given to structural racism as a central mechanism perpetuating health disparities in the United States. Dr. Williams builds on this emerging research by proposing a new conceptual model for studying Black-White inequality: The Three M's (The Making, Manifestation, and Maintenance) of Racial Inequality. His key argument is that the creation of socio-historical and political categories-specifically "race" -set in motion a mutually sustaining process of structural anti-Blackness and the preservation of Whiteness. These forces operate like interlocking gears that ensure the persistence of racial inequality. In this talk, Dr. Williams will focus on the four mechanisms that function as the metaphorical lubricant that ensures the gears spin perpetually. This entire process is shrouded in racial mystification that makes these socio-historical and political constructs (i.e., racial categories) and racial health disparities seem natural and inevitable. This event is FREE and open to the public. Register here for the Zoom link: https://ow.ly/hIo950Yj0tT Minority Health Disparities Initiative Status: CONFIRMED | -

events.title: Eugene Onegin (The Met: Live in HD)
events.location: Mary Riepma Ross Media Arts Center-Van Brunt Visitors Center Room:Ross Theaters (123 & 124)
events.description: Showing at The Ross on Saturday, May 2 at 12pm and Sunday, May 10 at 12pm. Visit theross.org for tickets and more information. Following her acclaimed 2024 company debut in Puccini's Madama Butterfly, soprano Asmik Grigorian returns to the Met as Tatiana, the lovestruck young heroine in this ardent operatic adaptation of Pushkin, which will be transmitted live from the Metropolitan Opera stage to cinemas worldwide on May 2. Baritone Igor Golovatenko reprises his portrayal of the urbane Onegin, who realizes his affection for her all too late. The Met's evocative production, directed by Tony Award-winner Deborah Warner, "offers a beautifully detailed reading of ... Tchaikovsky's lyrical romance" (The Telegraph). Ross EventStatus: CONFIRMED | |

events.title: Turner and Constable
events.location: Mary Riepma Ross Media Arts Center-Van Brunt Visitors Center Room:Ross Theater (Room 124)
events.description: Showing at The Ross on Sunday, May 3 at 1:30pm. Visit theross.org for tickets and more information. Celebrating the 250th anniversary of their births, this unmissable new documentary explores Turner and Constable's intertwined lives and legacies alongside the groundbreaking Tate exhibition. ALL TICKETS $10. Two of Britain's greatest painters, J.M.W. Turner and John Constable were also the greatest of rivals. Born within a year of each other, both used landscape painting to reflect the changing world around them. Tate Britain is bringing these two greats together for a groundbreaking exhibition, in London from November 2025 to April 2026, and Exhibition on Screen once again has exclusive and privileged access to bring their extraordinary art and remarkable stories to the big screen. Discover unexpected sides to both artists with intimate views of sketchbooks and personal items and insights from leading experts.Ross MovieStatus: CONFIRMED | |

Each session offers an opportunity to learn, reflect, and engage at your own comfort level - no pressure to share or participate beyond what feels comfortable to you. This is an open, drop-in group. Drop-In GroupStatus: CONFIRMED | - |

events.title: Suffs
events.description: Behind every powerful woman ... are more powerful women. Direct from Broadway, comes the acclaimed Tony Award®-winning musical Suffs about the brilliant, passionate, and funny American women who fought tirelessly for the right to vote. Created by Shaina Taub, the first woman to ever independently win Tony Awards for Best Book and Best Score in the same season, this “thrilling, inspiring and dazzlingly entertaining” (Variety) new musical boldly explores the triumphs and failures of a struggle for equality that’s far from over. Winner of the Outer Critics’ Circle Award for Best New Musical. “Like all of the best Broadway musicals, Suffs humanizes, empowers, moves, and entertains.” Chicago Tribune Recommended for ages 10+. Mature language. May 5, 2025 at 7:30 PM May 6, 2026 at 7:30 PM May 7, 2026 at 7:30 PM May 8, 2026 at 7:30 PM May 9, 2026 at 2:00 PM May 9, 2026 at 7:30 PM May 10, 2026 at 1:30 PM Status: CONFIRMED | - |

events.title: Grief Group
events.description: Grief can be an isolating experience. You don't have to go through it alone. CAPS is hosting a weekly Grief Group where all students can find a safe and supportive space to process the death of a friend, family member, or loved one with peers who have had similar experiences. events.title: Yi Yi (4K Restoration)
events.location: Mary Riepma Ross Media Arts Center-Van Brunt Visitors Center Room:Ross Theater (Room 124)
events.description: ONE NIGHT ONLY ~ Wednesday, May 6 @ 6:30 p.m. Visit theross.org for tickets and more information. The extraordinary, internationally embraced Yi Yi (A One and a Two . . .), directed by the late Taiwanese master Edward Yang, follows a middle-class family in Taipei over the course of one year, beginning with a wedding and ending with a funeral. Whether chronicling middle-age father NJ's tentative flirtations with an old flame or precocious young son Yang-Yang's attempts at capturing reality with his beloved camera, the filmmaker deftly imbues every gorgeous frame with a compassionate clarity. Warm, sprawling, and dazzling, this intimate epic is one of the undisputed masterworks of the new century. TICKETS: $10 General Admission | $5 Friends Members & UNL Students.Ross EventStatus: CONFIRMED | |

events.title: I Swear
events.location: Mary Riepma Ross Media Arts Center-Van Brunt Visitors Center Room:Ross Theaters (123 & 124)
events.description: Showing at The Ross April 24-May 7. Visit theross.org for tickets, showtimes, and more information. Kirk Jones' emotionally engaging, funny and compelling film charts John Davidson's Tourette's diagnosis at the age of 14 years old. Set within 1980s Britain, the story follows him throughout his troubled teens and early adulthood, and explores this little known and entirely misunderstood condition, along with his attempts to live a 'normal' life against the odds.Ross MovieStatus: CONFIRMED | |

events.title: Part 107 Remote Pilot Exam Prep Course
events.description: This 1-day (8-hour) course prepares students to pass the FAA Part 107 Aeronautical Knowledge Exam required to receive a remote pilot certificate (aka drone license). Covers aviation topics such as regulations, airspace, radio communication, weather, aircraft performance, and other basic aviation concepts necessary to pass the exam. Aviation experience is not essential to be successful in the course. This course is geared towards anyone interested in flying drones for commercial or public applications, including agriculture, spray drones, emergency management, law enforcement, real estate and land management, and photography. events.title: Eugene Onegin (The Met: Live in HD)
events.location: Mary Riepma Ross Media Arts Center-Van Brunt Visitors Center Room:Ross Theaters (123 & 124)
events.description: Showing at The Ross on Saturday, May 2 at 12pm and Sunday, May 10 at 12pm. Visit theross.org for tickets and more information. Following her acclaimed 2024 company debut in Puccini's Madama Butterfly, soprano Asmik Grigorian returns to the Met as Tatiana, the lovestruck young heroine in this ardent operatic adaptation of Pushkin, which will be transmitted live from the Metropolitan Opera stage to cinemas worldwide on May 2. Baritone Igor Golovatenko reprises his portrayal of the urbane Onegin, who realizes his affection for her all too late. The Met's evocative production, directed by Tony Award-winner Deborah Warner, "offers a beautifully detailed reading of ... Tchaikovsky's lyrical romance" (The Telegraph). Ross EventStatus: CONFIRMED | |

events.title: Native American Film Series
events.location: Mary Riepma Ross Media Arts Center-Van Brunt Visitors Center Room:Ross Theater (123)
events.description: Now in it's third year, the Native American Film Series at The Ross features free monthly screenings showcasing Native American films and television programs from the Vision Maker Media public broadcasting archives. Presented by The Ross Media Arts Center, Vision Maker Media, and Friends of The Ross. Unless otherwise noted, screenings in this series will take place on the second Monday of each month and are admission free and open to the public. Tickets available at the Ross box office (online ticketing not available for free screenings). Showing in April: SCHA'NEXW ELHTAL'NEXW SALMON PEOPLE: PRESERVING A WAY OF LIFE is an hour-long documentary inspired by the late Chexanexwh Larry Kinley, a Lummi fisherman and tribal leader who embodied a belief in tribal sovereignty. The film follows two Lummi families fishing for sockeye. As they come to grips with a depleting fishery, Larry asks: "Who Are We Without Salmon?" Celebrating the resilience and adaptive natures of salmon and the people, the film is a reflection on a spiritual life way centered on respect and gratitude for salmon. Ross EventStatus: CONFIRMED | |

events.title: Seminar Series: Katherine Keyes
events.description: Katherine Keyes, PhD, will present "Generational Differences in Substance Use Across the Lifecourse: Implications for Health and Prevention," at the May Seminar Series. She is a Professor of Epidemiology at the Columbia University Mailman School of Public Health. The incidence, prevalence and course of a wide range of drugs and alcohol use varies across generations. This variation reveals critical determinants of the social underpinnings of substance use etiology and has wide ranging implications for health for each generation as they progress through the lifecourse. In this talk, Dr. Keyes will provide an overview of cohort effects in substance use, discuss current trends, and illuminate how these generational differences can inform a prevention-focused approach to US health. This event is free and open to the public. Registration is required to receive the Zoom link: ow.ly/hyHr50Yj6Ae Rural Drug Addiction Research CenterStatus: CONFIRMED | -

events.title: Peace Corps Prep Application Deadline
events.description: Integrating UNL coursework, hands-on experience, and professional development, Nebraska’s Peace Corps Prep program prepares undergraduates for volunteer service globally, potentially with the Peace Corps, by building the following four core competencies: 1) Sector-specific skills (in Education, Health, Environment, Agriculture, Youth in Development, or Community Economic Development) 2) Foreign language proficiency 3) Intercultural competence 4) Professional savvy and leadership Upon completion of the program, you will receive a certificate from the Peace Corps, a federal agency. Completing the PC Prep program gives you a competitive edge when applying for Peace Corps service, but does not guarantee acceptance as a Peace Corps Volunteer. Special features of the PC Prep program at the University of Nebraska-Lincoln include: *Eligibility for competitive scholarship to study or intern abroad in a country where Peace Corps volunteers have served. *Tailored education abroad advising and Intercultural Development Inventory (IDI) assessment upon acceptance and completion. *Social/networking events, group volunteer experiences, and announcements about related campus and community events. *Opportunities to engage with Returned Peace Corps Volunteers in the community through events and mentorship. *Culminating zero-credit course listed on the transcript, recognition in campus media outlets, and a cord to wear at graduation. Learn more and start your application today!SummerStatus: CONFIRMED |

events.description: The ServSafe Manager course is a nationally accredited, 5-year food safety certification designed for foodservice managers. events.title: Lumière, Le Cinema!
events.location: Mary Riepma Ross Media Arts Center-Van Brunt Visitors Center Room:Ross Theater (Room 124)
events.description: ONE NIGHT ONLY ~ Wednesday, May 20 @ 7:30 p.m. Visit theross.org for tickets, showtimes, and more information. In one of those wonderful coincidences of history, lumire, the French word for "light," was also the last name of brothers Auguste and Louis, whose brilliant invention, the cinematograph, helped to inaugurate the most beloved art form of the last 130 years. Institute Lumire director Thierry Frmaux uses Lumire, Le Cinema! to guide the viewer through over a hundred shorts-some famous, some forgotten, some never before seen-directed by Lumire and company. In the process, Frmaux illuminates how the brothers employed the camera as a creative instrument as they (and their operators) mastered framing, staging, and subject selection for quotidian and exotic microdocumentaries as well as the first ever fictional motion pictures. The result is not only a glorious re(telling) of the genesis of cinema but a profound meditation on the beautiful world captured-and the mysterious world imagined-by the Lumires. TICKETS: $10 General Admission | $5 Friends Members & UNL Students.Ross EventStatus: CONFIRMED | |

events.title: NRICH: From Impersonal Network to a Meaningful Community — Thriving as a Young Professional
events.description: "How do you find your people? While LinkedIn, networking events and coffee meetings are fun and can be helpful tools to grow your network, they may not be making the lasting impact you are searching for. "From Impersonal Network to a Meaningful Community: Thriving as a Young Professional" helps young professionals demystify how they can build the right positive relationships to become a connected and community-minded individual. We'll encourage young careerists to balance the quantity and quality of their connections and give strategies for extroverts and introverts alike. We use our original framework of Discover, Engage and Advance to help participants set individualized goals related to community building. Attendees will leave this interactive workshop empowered to use actionable strategies to be a positive and impactful member of their community!" Presenters: Natalie Becerra, Career Development Specialist * Journalism & Mass Comm Lizzie Vietz, Marketing Strategist * Student Life Marketing & Comm The University of Nebraska-Lincoln is committed to accessibility in our events and programs. We provide accommodations for individuals with disabilities to participate effectively and safely. Every week throughout the growing season, we are here to answer all of your gardening and landscape questions. Our weekly advice is tailored to the gardening season, addressing weather challenges and tasks relevant at different times of the year. This helps gardeners know what to do and when to do it, from planting and pruning to lawn care and pest control. Our experts will help you diagnose problems and offer effective, environmentally friendly solutions. Backyard Farmer Television showStatus: CONFIRMED | -

events.title: Part 107 Remote Pilot Exam Prep Course
events.description: This 1-day (8-hour) course prepares students to pass the FAA Part 107 Aeronautical Knowledge Exam required to receive a remote pilot certificate (aka drone license). Covers aviation topics such as regulations, airspace, radio communication, weather, aircraft performance, and other basic aviation concepts necessary to pass the exam. Aviation experience is not essential to be successful in the course. This course is geared towards anyone interested in flying drones for commercial or public applications, including agriculture, spray drones, emergency management, law enforcement, real estate and land management, and photography. One-Day Drone Training from Nebraska ExtensionStatus: CONFIRMED | - |
events.group: One-Day Drone Training from Nebraska Extension

events.title: Herman's Hermits Starring Peter Noone
events.location: Lied Center for Performing Arts Room:Lied Center Main Stage
events.description: Universally regarded as one of rock and roll’s finest and most versatile entertainers, Peter Noone is second to none! Star of stage, screen, and record, Noone’s performances are the stuff of legend, deftly delivering a broad palette of music that defined a generation. Herman’s Hermits starring Peter Noone is a must-see concert featuring some of the biggest hits of the 1960s, including “I’m Into Something Good,” “I’m Henry VIII, I Am,” “There’s a Kind of Hush,” “Mrs. Brown You’ve Got a Lovely Daughter,” and many more. With 20 top 40 hits and over 60 million records sold, Peter Noone is the real deal.Status: CONFIRMED | |

events.title: The Science of Ice Cream Camp
events.description: Young Nebraska Scientists (YNS) conducted by Nebraska EPSCoR with funding from the U.S. National Science Foundation (NSF) - offers camps for middle and high school students throughout the state. YNS experiences focus on Science, Technology, Engineering and Math (STEM) topics, and are a great way for teens to sample a career, meet friends and mentors, and find their future! NSF fundings helps keep YNS camp fees low; and need-based scholarships are readily available. Faculty from Nebraska colleges and universities are camp leaders, and camp staff include university students. Students will learn about ice cream production through hands-on activities in the Food Innovation Center on the Nebraska Innovation Campus. This camp is great for students interested in chemistry, microbiology, and/or emulsification. Come discover all the incredible science behind food production. Science never tasted so good! Camp fee is $20 per student. Apply: https://yns.nebraska.edu/gain-hands-on-experience-in-stem/summer-camps. Early application deadline is May 1. Priority is given to high-school students. After May 1, middle-school students may apply, if space is available.Status: CONFIRMED | - |

events.title: The Playboy of the Western World (National Theatre)
events.location: Mary Riepma Ross Media Arts Center-Van Brunt Visitors Center Room:Ross Theaters (123 & 124)
events.description: Showing at The Ross on Thursday, May 28 at 7:15pm and Sunday, May 31 at 1:00pm. Visit theross.org for tickets and more information. Nicola Coughlan (Bridgerton) joins anna Hardwicke (The Sixth Commandment) and Siobhn McSweeney (Derry Girls) in John Millington Synge's riveting play of youth and self-discovery. Pegeen Flaherty's life is turned upside down when a young man walks into her pub claiming that he's killed his father. Instead of being shunned, the killer becomes a local hero and begins to win hearts, that is until a second man unexpectedly arrives on the scene... Filmed live on stage at the National Theatre, Caitrona McLaughlin directs this darkly funny tale full to the brim with secrets.Ross EventStatus: CONFIRMED | |

events.title: El Ultimo Sueño de Frida y Diego (The Met: Live in HD)
events.location: Mary Riepma Ross Media Arts Center-Van Brunt Visitors Center Room:Ross Theaters (123 & 124)
events.description: Showing at The Ross on Saturday, May 30 at 12pm and Sunday, June 7 at 12pm. Visit theross.org for tickets and more information. The Metropolitan Opera's 2025-26 Live in HD season comes to a close with a live transmission of American composer Gabriela Lena Frank's first opera, a magical-realist portrait of Mexico's painterly power couple Frida Kahlo and Diego Rivera, with libretto by Pulitzer Prize-winning playwright Nilo Cruz. Fashioned as a reversal of the Orpheus and Euridice myth, the story depicts Frida, sung by leading mezzo-soprano Isabel Leonard, leaving the underworld on the Day of the Dead and reuniting with Diego, portrayed by baritone Carlos lvarez. The famously feuding pair briefly relive their tumultuous love, embracing both the passion and the pain before bidding the land of the living a final farewell. Music Director Yannick Nzet-Sguin conducts the Met-premiere staging of Frank's opera, a "confident, richly imagined score" (The New Yorker) that "bursts with color and fresh individuality" (Los Angeles Times). The vibrant new production, taking enthusiastic inspiration from Frida and Diego's paintings, is directed and choreographed by Deborah Colker. Ross EventStatus: CONFIRMED | |

events.title: High School Workshop
events.description: Our interactive workshop, developed for high school students at all levels, provides a unique experience to investigate issues surrounding design and learn more about the design professions of architecture, landscape architecture and interior design. The workshop provides learning opportunities in the form of discussions, design studio explorations and video presentations. The central focus of the workshop is a design studio where students explore design issues and nurture creative energies. As part of the workshop students will learn about architectural conventions, practice client/designer scenarios, explore various career opportunities, virtually visit architecturally significant buildings and iconic landscapes and develop fundamental skills necessary for the study of design. Who Can Participate? Students who have completed two or more years of their high school education and have an interest in architecture, landscape architecture or interior design are encouraged to apply to the workshop. You do not need courses in art or drafting in order to participate. We have reserved space for 36 students the workshop, so register early. How Much Will It Cost? For the Career Explorations Workshop, the fee is $650 per participant. This includes studio supplies, program fees, meals and lodging. Students participating in this residential program are supervised overnight while in the residence hall by College of Architecture staff. For more information visit: https://architecture.unl.edu/high-school-camp Status: CONFIRMED | - |

events.title: Native American Film Series
events.location: Mary Riepma Ross Media Arts Center-Van Brunt Visitors Center Room:Ross Theater (123)
events.description: Now in it's third year, the Native American Film Series at The Ross features free monthly screenings showcasing Native American films and television programs from the Vision Maker Media public broadcasting archives. Presented by The Ross Media Arts Center, Vision Maker Media, and Friends of The Ross. Unless otherwise noted, screenings in this series will take place on the second Monday of each month and are admission free and open to the public. Tickets available at the Ross box office (online ticketing not available for free screenings). Showing in April: SCHA'NEXW ELHTAL'NEXW SALMON PEOPLE: PRESERVING A WAY OF LIFE is an hour-long documentary inspired by the late Chexanexwh Larry Kinley, a Lummi fisherman and tribal leader who embodied a belief in tribal sovereignty. The film follows two Lummi families fishing for sockeye. As they come to grips with a depleting fishery, Larry asks: "Who Are We Without Salmon?" Celebrating the resilience and adaptive natures of salmon and the people, the film is a reflection on a spiritual life way centered on respect and gratitude for salmon. Ross EventStatus: CONFIRMED | |

events.title: New Employee Orientation
events.description: You will receive valuable insights and resources during your orientation. • Get acquainted with the vibrant community and inclusive culture at the university. • Connect with fellow new employees from various departments across campus and engage in meaningful conversations. • Dive into our strategic plan and explore how it aligns with your role and goals within the university. • Gain valuable insights from a senior leader who will share their experiences and vision for the university’s future. This session will also provide an opportunity for you to ask questions. • Learn about the comprehensive benefits package available to you as an employee of the university. Students who have completed two or more years of their high school education and have an interest in architecture, landscape architecture or interior design are encouraged to apply to the workshop. You do not need courses in art or drafting in order to participate. We have reserved space for 36 students the workshop, so register early. How Much Will It Cost? For the Career Explorations Workshop, the fee is $650 per participant. This includes studio supplies, program fees, meals and lodging. Students participating in this residential program are supervised overnight while in the residence hall by College of Architecture staff. For more information visit: https://architecture.unl.edu/high-school-camp Status: CONFIRMED | - |

events.title: Rise and Grind: A Wheat Tale from Field to Flour and Beyond Camp
events.description: Young Nebraska Scientists (YNS) conducted by Nebraska EPSCoR with funding from the U.S. National Science Foundation (NSF) - offers camps for middle and high school students throughout the state. YNS experiences focus on Science, Technology, Engineering and Math (STEM) topics, and are a great way for teens to sample a career, meet friends and mentors, and find their future! NSF fundings helps keep YNS camp fees low; and need-based scholarships are readily available. Faculty from Nebraska colleges and universities are camp leaders, and camp staff include university students. Rise and Grind is a four-day immersive food science camp that follows wheat from the field to the foods we eat every day. Through farm visits, drying, milling, baking, and nutritional exploration, students experience wheat as a resilient material shaped by environment, force, and transformation. This camp blends agriculture, engineering, chemistry, and health sciences to show how processing with purpose turns grain into flour, bread, cookies, and other staple foods. Ideal for students interested in food systems, sustainability, and the science behind everyday foods. Camp fee is $150. Financial scholarships are available! There is a discounted rate for local students who would like to stay at home and commute. Apply: https://yns.nebraska.edu/gain-hands-on-experience-in-stem/summer-campsStatus: CONFIRMED | - |

events.title: Les Liaisons Dangereuses (National Theatre)
events.location: Mary Riepma Ross Media Arts Center-Van Brunt Visitors Center Room:Ross Theaters (123 & 124)
events.description: Showing at The Ross Thursday, June 25 at 7:15pm and Sunday June 28 at 1:00pm. Visit theross.org for tickets and more information. BAFTA Award-winner Lesley Manville (Phantom Thread) joins Aidan Turner (Rivals) in a striking new staging of Christopher Hampton's celebrated adaptation of the classic novel, where among the glittering salons of the super-rich, one misstep can mean ruin. Marquise de Merteuil is a master in the art of survival. Alongside the magnetic Vicomte de Valmont, they turn seduction into strategy and weaponize desire. But when their alliance collapses into rivalry, the battle between them threatens to destroy everyone in their path. Filmed live on stage at the National Theatre, Marianne Elliott (Angels in America) directs this thrilling game of love, lies, and social warfare.Ross EventStatus: CONFIRMED | |

events.title: The Science of Chocolate Camp
events.description: Young Nebraska Scientists (YNS) conducted by Nebraska EPSCoR with funding from the U.S. National Science Foundation (NSF) - offers camps for middle and high school students throughout the state. YNS experiences focus on Science, Technology, Engineering and Math (STEM) topics, and are a great way for teens to sample a career, meet friends and mentors, and find their future! NSF fundings helps keep YNS camp fees low; and need-based scholarships are readily available. Faculty from Nebraska colleges and universities are camp leaders, and camp staff include university students. People have been obsessed with chocolate for over 5,000 years. The Olmec and Mayan people used cocoa beans to make chocolatey drinks. The Aztecs used cocoa beans as currency and to make tasty treats. However, the first chocolate bar didn't show up until 1847 and has only gotten better since. This camp is great for students interested in fermentation, roasting, and finding out how we can change the texture and flavor of this amazing treat. Join us to discover the incredible science behind chocolate production. Science never tasted so good! The camp fee is $20. Register: https://yns.nebraska.edu/gain-hands-on-experience-in-stem/summer-camps Early application deadline is May 1. Priority is given to high-school students. After May 1, middle-school students may apply, if space is available.Status: CONFIRMED | - |

events.title: Native American Film Series
events.location: Mary Riepma Ross Media Arts Center-Van Brunt Visitors Center Room:Ross Theater (123)
events.description: Now in it's third year, the Native American Film Series at The Ross features free monthly screenings showcasing Native American films and television programs from the Vision Maker Media public broadcasting archives. Presented by The Ross Media Arts Center, Vision Maker Media, and Friends of The Ross. Unless otherwise noted, screenings in this series will take place on the second Monday of each month and are admission free and open to the public. Tickets available at the Ross box office (online ticketing not available for free screenings). Showing in April: SCHA'NEXW ELHTAL'NEXW SALMON PEOPLE: PRESERVING A WAY OF LIFE is an hour-long documentary inspired by the late Chexanexwh Larry Kinley, a Lummi fisherman and tribal leader who embodied a belief in tribal sovereignty. The film follows two Lummi families fishing for sockeye. As they come to grips with a depleting fishery, Larry asks: "Who Are We Without Salmon?" Celebrating the resilience and adaptive natures of salmon and the people, the film is a reflection on a spiritual life way centered on respect and gratitude for salmon. Ross EventStatus: CONFIRMED | |

events.title: ServSafe® Training
events.description: The purpose of the ServSafe(R) Food Safety Program is to help food service operations protect public health by preventing foodborne illness and promoting safe food-handling practices. In response to increased publicity surrounding foodborne illness outbreaks and growing consumer concern, this program provides nationally recognized, science-based training developed by the National Restaurant Association. Through ServSafe(R) Manager and ServSafe(R) Food Handler training, participants gain the knowledge and skills needed to provide safe food, reduce contamination risks, and maintain clean, sanitary facilities. The program prepares managers and employees to implement effective food safety management systems, follow proper purchasing, storage, preparation, service procedures, cleaning and sanitation, and train staff in best practices. Intended Audience: The ServSafe(R) Food Safety Program, developed by the National Restaurant Association, addresses growing food safety concerns by providing science-based training for food service professionals. ServSafe(R) Manager is designed for food service managers and supervisors in restaurants, hospitals, schools, long-term care facilities, and other food-handling operations. It offers nationally recognized certification and covers current food safety principles and best practices. ServSafe(R) Food Handler is a basic food safety course for food service employees. It covers essential topics including personal hygiene, cross-contamination and allergens, time and temperature control, and cleaning and sanitation. Certification: By successfully completing the ServSafe(R) Food Safety Program training, food service professionals can earn National Restaurant Association certification, update their food safety skills using the latest FDA Food Code guidance, and strengthen their operation's commitment to food safety and customer confidence.In-PersonStatus: CONFIRMED | - |

events.title: NRICH: Husker Hospitality
events.description: Learn the strategies for delivering excellent service by emphasizing understanding customer needs and exceeding their expectations. Learn the importance of recognizing all interaction points and proactively anticipating customer needs. You'll receive practical advice on effective communication, including active listening, using clear language, and offering helpful alternatives. Furthermore, you'll learn a process for resolving conflicts by emphasizing empathy, listening, action, and restoring customer satisfaction. You'll come away with actionable steps to create positive and memorable service experiences. Presented by Ranelle Maltas, Training, Technology Specialist Biography: Ranelle Maltas has been a dedicated member of the University of Nebraska-Lincoln (UNL) community since 2000, when she began her journey as an Application Trainer for Business & Finance. From 2007 to 2018, she served as the Learning & Development Manager for ITS, spearheading initiatives to enhance technology learning. In September 2018, Ranelle transitioned to the Office of Human Resources, where she took on a multifaceted role developing and presenting training programs, coordinating the New Employee Orientation, and overseeing the HR website. Currently, as a key contributor to HR's Organizational Development & Training team, Ranelle focuses on designing and coordinating professional development opportunities to empower staff and foster growth across UNL. Beyond her professional work, Ranelle is an active member of the community. She serves on the Board of Directors of the UNL Band Alumni Association and is the Vice President of Technology for the National Association of Alumni Bands. Additionally, she shares her expertise by teaching Excel courses for the SCC Continuing Education Center. On a personal note, Ranelle is a proud mother of a UNL graduate, a devoted wife to Bill, and co-owner of the Waffleman(TM). . Click the link below to register:Status: CONFIRMED | -

events.title: Snack Attack: Apple Edition Camp
events.description: Young Nebraska Scientists (YNS) conducted by Nebraska EPSCoR with funding from the U.S. National Science Foundation (NSF) - offers camps for middle and high school students throughout the state. YNS experiences focus on Science, Technology, Engineering and Math (STEM) topics, and are a great way for teens to sample a career, meet friends and mentors, and find their future! NSF fundings helps keep YNS camp fees low; and need-based scholarships are readily available. Faculty from Nebraska colleges and universities are camp leaders, and camp staff include university students. This is a hands-on, two-day food science camp where students explore how simple, recognizable ingredients can be intentionally processed into smart, functional snacks. Working in a clean, design-focused lab environment, students deconstruct apples to understand their structure, transform them using different processing methods, and reflect on how food design choices affect health and sustainability. This camp is ideal for students curious about food science, clean-label products, sustainability, and how everyday snacks are engineered with purpose. This camp is sponsored by the Kimmel Foundation. Camp fee is $40. Apply: https://yns.nebraska.edu/gain-hands-on-experience-in-stem/summer-camps. Early application deadline is May 1. Priority is given to high-school students. After May 1, middle-school students may apply, if space is available.Status: CONFIRMED | - |

Camp fee is $40. Apply: https://yns.nebraska.edu/gain-hands-on-experience-in-stem/summer-camps. Early application deadline is May 1. Priority is given to high-school students. After May 1, middle-school students may apply, if space is available.Status: CONFIRMED | - |

events.title: Native American Film Series
events.location: Mary Riepma Ross Media Arts Center-Van Brunt Visitors Center Room:Ross Theater (123)
events.description: Now in it's third year, the Native American Film Series at The Ross features free monthly screenings showcasing Native American films and television programs from the Vision Maker Media public broadcasting archives. Presented by The Ross Media Arts Center, Vision Maker Media, and Friends of The Ross. Unless otherwise noted, screenings in this series will take place on the second Monday of each month and are admission free and open to the public. Tickets available at the Ross box office (online ticketing not available for free screenings). Showing in April: SCHA'NEXW ELHTAL'NEXW SALMON PEOPLE: PRESERVING A WAY OF LIFE is an hour-long documentary inspired by the late Chexanexwh Larry Kinley, a Lummi fisherman and tribal leader who embodied a belief in tribal sovereignty. The film follows two Lummi families fishing for sockeye. As they come to grips with a depleting fishery, Larry asks: "Who Are We Without Salmon?" Celebrating the resilience and adaptive natures of salmon and the people, the film is a reflection on a spiritual life way centered on respect and gratitude for salmon. Ross EventStatus: CONFIRMED | |

events.title: Peace Corps Prep Application Deadline
events.description: Integrating UNL coursework, hands-on experience, and professional development, Nebraska’s Peace Corps Prep program prepares undergraduates for volunteer service globally, potentially with the Peace Corps, by building the following four core competencies: 1) Sector-specific skills (in Education, Health, Environment, Agriculture, Youth in Development, or Community Economic Development) 2) Foreign language proficiency 3) Intercultural competence 4) Professional savvy and leadership Upon completion of the program, you will receive a certificate from the Peace Corps, a federal agency. Completing the PC Prep program gives you a competitive edge when applying for Peace Corps service, but does not guarantee acceptance as a Peace Corps Volunteer. Special features of the PC Prep program at the University of Nebraska-Lincoln include: *Eligibility for competitive scholarship to study or intern abroad in a country where Peace Corps volunteers have served. *Tailored education abroad advising and Intercultural Development Inventory (IDI) assessment upon acceptance and completion. *Social/networking events, group volunteer experiences, and announcements about related campus and community events. *Opportunities to engage with Returned Peace Corps Volunteers in the community through events and mentorship. *Culminating zero-credit course listed on the transcript, recognition in campus media outlets, and a cord to wear at graduation. Learn more and start your application today!FallStatus: CONFIRMED |

events.title: Health Equity Grand Rounds: Ciera Kirkpatrick
events.description: Ciera Kirkpatrick, PhD, will join us for our October Grand Rounds to discuss "Scrolling Through Motherhood: How Social Media Shapes Maternal Mental Health." Today's mothers are navigating motherhood in a world saturated with social media. From carefully curated parenting posts on Instagram to postpartum "bounce back" fitspiration videos on TikTok, social media is exacerbating the culture of comparison and shaping maternal mental health in ways we are only beginning to understand. In this talk, Dr. Kirkpatrick will discuss findings from a series of experiments examining how idealized social media content influences mothers' anxiety, shame, body image, and confidence in their parenting abilities. She will also discuss a promising strategy for reducing the harms of social media comparisons. This talk will bring attention to social media's effects on mental health and what we can do about it. Dr. Kirkpatrick is an Assistant Professor of Advertising and Public Relations at the University of Nebraska-Lincoln College of Journalism and Mass Communications. Her research uses a social scientific experimental approach to investigate how messaging in the media (e.g., advertising, news, social media content) affects individuals' mental and physical health and how strategic communicators can effectively design messages to improve individuals' health outcomes. Learn more about Dr. Kirkpatrick: https://journalism.unl.edu/person/ciera-kirkpatrick/ This event is FREE and open to the public. Register here for the Zoom link: https://unl.zoom.us/webinar/register/WN_MefhgDCBSey4eFd_f4h6sg#/registration Minority Health Disparities Initiative Status: CONFIRMED | -

events.title: ServSafe® Training
events.description: The purpose of the ServSafe(R) Food Safety Program is to help food service operations protect public health by preventing foodborne illness and promoting safe food-handling practices. In response to increased publicity surrounding foodborne illness outbreaks and growing consumer concern, this program provides nationally recognized, science-based training developed by the National Restaurant Association. Through ServSafe(R) Manager and ServSafe(R) Food Handler training, participants gain the knowledge and skills needed to provide safe food, reduce contamination risks, and maintain clean, sanitary facilities. The program prepares managers and employees to implement effective food safety management systems, follow proper purchasing, storage, preparation, service procedures, cleaning and sanitation, and train staff in best practices. Intended Audience: The ServSafe(R) Food Safety Program, developed by the National Restaurant Association, addresses growing food safety concerns by providing science-based training for food service professionals. ServSafe(R) Manager is designed for food service managers and supervisors in restaurants, hospitals, schools, long-term care facilities, and other food-handling operations. It offers nationally recognized certification and covers current food safety principles and best practices. ServSafe(R) Food Handler is a basic food safety course for food service employees. It covers essential topics including personal hygiene, cross-contamination and allergens, time and temperature control, and cleaning and sanitation. Certification: By successfully completing the ServSafe(R) Food Safety Program training, food service professionals can earn National Restaurant Association certification, update their food safety skills using the latest FDA Food Code guidance, and strengthen their operation's commitment to food safety and customer confidence.In-PersonStatus: CONFIRMED | - |

events.title: Nebraska Innovation Campus Blood Drive
events.description: American Red Cross will be at Nebraska Innovation Campus on Wednesday, October 21 from 10:00 a.m. - 3:00 p.m. NIC Partners and Lincoln community are encouraged to donate blood. Every day, blood donors help patients of all ages: accident and burn victims, heart surgery and organ transplant patients, and those battling cancer. In fact, every two seconds, someone in the U.S. needs blood. To make an appointment to donate blood, visit: https://www.redcrossblood.org/give.html/drive-results?zipSponsor=NebraskaInnovationCampus. The blood drive will take place in The Club, the private event space connected to The Mill Coffee & Bistro.Status: CONFIRMED | - |

events.title: Staff Senate District 7: Research District-Wide Meeting
events.description: Staff members are invited to attend the upcoming District 7 Staff Senate district-wide meeting! This is a vital opportunity to connect directly with your Staff Senators, receive updates on current campus initiatives, and contribute your voice to important discussions that affect the UNL staff community. District 7 Open Houses from 10-11am quarterly on third Wednesdays in 2026 January 21: East Campus Prairie A April 15: City Campus Union Chimney Rock Room July 15: Innovation Campus Board Room October 21: Whittier 152 Virtual access: https://unl.zoom.us/j/93631255766 Your Voice Matters! Join us to hear the latest from the Senate, ask questions, share your feedback, concerns, and ideas, and network with colleagues. Let’s work together to ensure effective staff representation and make UNL an even better workplace.Status: CONFIRMED | - |

events.title: Part 107 Remote Pilot Exam Prep Course
events.description: This 1-day (8-hour) course prepares students to pass the FAA Part 107 Aeronautical Knowledge Exam required to receive a remote pilot certificate (aka drone license). Covers aviation topics such as regulations, airspace, radio communication, weather, aircraft performance, and other basic aviation concepts necessary to pass the exam. Aviation experience is not essential to be successful in the course. This course is geared towards anyone interested in flying drones for commercial or public applications, including agriculture, spray drones, emergency management, law enforcement, real estate and land management, and photography. One-Day Drone Training from Nebraska ExtensionStatus: CONFIRMED | - |

events.title: Native American Film Series
events.location: Mary Riepma Ross Media Arts Center-Van Brunt Visitors Center Room:Ross Theater (123)
events.description: Now in it's third year, the Native American Film Series at The Ross features free monthly screenings showcasing Native American films and television programs from the Vision Maker Media public broadcasting archives. Presented by The Ross Media Arts Center, Vision Maker Media, and Friends of The Ross. Unless otherwise noted, screenings in this series will take place on the second Monday of each month and are admission free and open to the public. Tickets available at the Ross box office (online ticketing not available for free screenings). Showing in April: SCHA'NEXW ELHTAL'NEXW SALMON PEOPLE: PRESERVING A WAY OF LIFE is an hour-long documentary inspired by the late Chexanexwh Larry Kinley, a Lummi fisherman and tribal leader who embodied a belief in tribal sovereignty. The film follows two Lummi families fishing for sockeye. As they come to grips with a depleting fishery, Larry asks: "Who Are We Without Salmon?" Celebrating the resilience and adaptive natures of salmon and the people, the film is a reflection on a spiritual life way centered on respect and gratitude for salmon. Ross EventStatus: CONFIRMED | |

events.title: Peace Corps Prep Application Deadline
events.description: Integrating UNL coursework, hands-on experience, and professional development, Nebraska’s Peace Corps Prep program prepares undergraduates for volunteer service globally, potentially with the Peace Corps, by building the following four core competencies: 1) Sector-specific skills (in Education, Health, Environment, Agriculture, Youth in Development, or Community Economic Development) 2) Foreign language proficiency 3) Intercultural competence 4) Professional savvy and leadership Upon completion of the program, you will receive a certificate from the Peace Corps, a federal agency. Completing the PC Prep program gives you a competitive edge when applying for Peace Corps service, but does not guarantee acceptance as a Peace Corps Volunteer. Special features of the PC Prep program at the University of Nebraska-Lincoln include: *Eligibility for competitive scholarship to study or intern abroad in a country where Peace Corps volunteers have served. *Tailored education abroad advising and Intercultural Development Inventory (IDI) assessment upon acceptance and completion. *Social/networking events, group volunteer experiences, and announcements about related campus and community events. *Opportunities to engage with Returned Peace Corps Volunteers in the community through events and mentorship. *Culminating zero-credit course listed on the transcript, recognition in campus media outlets, and a cord to wear at graduation. Learn more and start your application today!SpringStatus: CONFIRMED |

events.title: Suicide Prevention w/ UNL CAPS
events.description: Join us for a Suicide Prevention Workshop, hosted by UNL CAPS. PREVENT SUICIDE is a 60-minute in-person training for University of Nebraska-Lincoln students, faculty and staff. Increase Your Knowledge: learn the warning signs associated with suicide. Name the Concerns and Ask: increase your confidence about communicating openly about suicide. Provide Support and Resources: expand awareness about resources. Connect To Help: learn how to take action when supporting others.

events.description: monthly dinner with food fromthe kosher kitchen

events.title: Created Concert
events.description: On March 27, 2026 at 6:00 pm, The Lutheran Center will host singer, songwriter, and musician Semler and the Muchachos Food Truck on our front lawn for an evening of live music and delicious food. Admission to the concert and food are free for EVERYONE. All are invited to join for great music, tasty food, and time spent with an affirming community. One of The Lutheran Center's foundational identities is as a Reconciling in Christ worshipping community. Adorned with a pride flag in our window and a welcome statement on our wall, we welcome students from all races, religions, cultures, and sexualities to worship and dwell within our community. The Lutheran Center remains committed to extending our wholehearted welcome to students, faculty, and staff on campus and folks from around the Lincoln community to experience the fullness of God’s unconditional love.

events.description: Meet us at the Union this Friday (27th) for a fun trivia night! The theme is “Are You Smarter Than a 5th Grader?” so get ready for some lighthearted competition and laughs. Come join the Simple Truth group for a Friday night filled with fellowship, friendly challenges, and plenty of fun.

events.title: Cultural Performance Night
events.description: A vibrant evening celebrating cultures from around the world. Join us for a dynamic showcase of performances by diverse RSOs and a special feature from Proyecto Cultural. Experience the energy, artistry, and traditions of global dance, and enjoy free cultural appetizers as you explore flavors from different communities. Come see, taste, and celebrate culture all in one unforgettable night!

events.title: Harmonies for Heroes: Shave for the Brave
events.description: Join NRHH for their annual Shave for the Brave fundraiser, raising money for St. Baldrick's, a childhood cancer foundation! At this exciting event, performances will be given by five amazing A Capella groups: The Bathtub Dogs, Take Note, Boots and Cats, The Red Keys, and Pitch Please! In between each set, we will have people shave their heads in solidarity with the children fighting childhood cancer. Tickets will be sold at booths around campus throughout the months up to the day of the event. Buying a ticket before will cost $5 for UNL students and $7 for the public. If you buy them at the door, they will be $7 for students and the public. All funds raised will be donated directly to the St. Baldrick's Foundation.

events.title: Engage Lincoln: People City Mission
events.location: People City Mission Help Center - 6800 P St, Lincoln, NE 68510.
events.description: People’s City Mission was started in 1907 by local churches, offering food and shelter for transient and impoverished men, women and children. This volunteer opportunity will involve sorting clothes and distributing items. Plan to meet at located at: Help Center - 6800 P St, Lincoln, NE 68510. There will be a door that says “Staff and Volunteer Entrance” please make sure to come in through this door. The Engage Lincoln program enables UNL students to volunteer at a Lincoln base nonprofit organization connected to addressing community needs. Learn More about ENGAGE LINCOLN week in September follow this link: https://involved.unl.edu/engage-lincoln If you have questions, reach out to Andrew Brown via email at andrew.brown@unl.edu

events.title: Huskers Go Nuclear Conference
events.description: This Conference will have 4 panels with 3 speakers each discussing many different nuclear related topics (SMR's, new nuclear reactors, spent nuclear fuel, etc.). It will last from 9am to 5pm, and students will be allowed in for free. Several guests have already confirmed their attendance, including representatives from Nebraska Public Power District, Advanced Nuclear Coalition, Bostleman Engineering Inc., TerraPower, and NUTech, along with other companies and organizations from across the Midwest region. Most of these representatives will be giving presentations based on their expertise. This conference will provide the general UNL student body with a valuable opportunity to learn about the current state of green energy and nuclear energy innovation. Students will encounter professionals who will assist them in creating their professional network while they explore different green energy production career options.

Here is our tentative event calendar: 01/20: Welcome Event 02/03: Career Services and Resume Review Session 02/17: Black History Month Celebration 03/03: Olsson Visit + National Conference Prep 03/31: Company Panel 04/14: Election 04/28: Community building TBD: Emotional Wellbeing (Abbott), Physical Activity event, and volunteering at Malone Center

events.title: She Dared to Dream: Screening and Panel Discussion
events.description: Be inspired by a story of courage, representation and transformative leadership. Join us for a special screening of She Dared to Dream: Ayanna Pressley, a documentary highlighting the historic journey of Ayanna Pressley (the first Black woman elected to Congress from Massachusetts) and her commitment to courageous leadership and meaningful policy change. Following the screening, stay for a moderated panel discussion featuring campus and community leaders. Together, we will explore themes of leadership, advocacy, resilience, identity and the power of representation in shaping inclusive communities. This event is an opportunity to:- Reflect on the impact of representation in public life.- Gain insight into leadership that challenges systems and expands opportunity.- Engage in thoughtful dialogue about identity, equity and civic participation. Whether you are passionate about public service, leadership, social change or personal growth - this evening will leave you informed, inspired and empowered. Come watch. Come learn. Come be part of the conversation. RSVP & Catering: Light appetizers will be provided. To help us ensure an accurate estimate and minimize food waste, please be intentional with your commitment and register at the link below: Register Here: https://go.unl.edu/shedaredtodream

events.title: Shotokan Ohshima Karate-do & Self-Defense
events.description: Please see our website and Google calendar for the most up-to-date practice schedule: https://unl.ska.org/karate-class-schedule/ Shotokan Ohshima is a very traditional style and differs from many martial arts schools. Comprehensive, challenging exercise is only one of the many benefits of karate practice. Teaching traditional karate-do as a serious art and way of life means much more. It ensures the original ideas of the founder are passed on to our students, so that we grow in all aspects of our lives. Altman Senpai pushes us to challenge ourselves and taste our life, to be more fully aware and conscientious at all times. Our karate practice can be very difficult. The main difficulty lies in training one's mind to be disciplined and focused. Remember, anyone can be lazy, because that is the easiest thing to do! Karate may be defined as a weapon-less means of self-defense. These techniques use all parts of the body to their maximum advantage. Our fundamental techniques are divided into three main aspects: - Kihon — Fundamental blocks, punches, kicks, and stances- Kata — Pre-arranged forms simulating combat situations- Kumite — Sparring In each category, the beginner is given instruction at the most basic level until the techniques become spontaneous to them. As the student progresses technically, the practice progresses physically and the student begins to build greater stamina. At this stage, one involves himself or herself with the more intricate and difficult kata and more dynamic forms of kumite. Karate as self-defense is one of the most dynamic of all the martial arts. The trained karate student is able to coordinate the mind and body perfectly, thereby allowing one to unleash tremendous physical power at will. It is not the possession of great physical strength that makes a strong karateka; rather it is the ability to coordinate the mind and body. Upon developing this ability, even the smallest person finds that within themselves is the power to deliver a devastating blow to any would-be attacker. The values of karate to modern life are numerous. In our everyday lives we often forget the value of exercise to both our physical and mental health. The practice of karate tones the body, develops coordination, quickens reflexes, and builds stamina. Also, the serious practice of karate develops composure, a clearer thought process, deeper insight into one's mental capabilities, and more self-confidence. In this, karate is not an end, but a means to an end. It is an activity in which advancing age is not a hindrance. Rather, it encourages proficiency and the keen coordination of mind and body.

events.title: Komensky Club One Life Film Screening
events.location: Red Cloud B-C located in the Willa Cather Dining Center
events.description: We will show the film One Life to our club members in Red Cloud B-C located in the Willa Cather Dining Center. One Life tells the story of London broker Nicholas "Nicky" Winton, who helps rescue hundreds of predominantly Jewish children from Czechoslovakia in a race against time before the Nazi occupation closes the borders. Fifty years later, he's still haunted by the fate of those he wasn't able to bring to safety.

Learn what our brotherhood is all about, ask questions about recruitment, meet current members, and see if Lambda Chi Alpha at UNL is the right fit for you. Lambda Chi Alpha is more than a fraternity. It is a brotherhood built on leadership, growth, service, and lifelong friendships. With over 300,000 initiated members nationwide, we focus on building strong men who make an impact on campus and beyond. Whether you are curious about Greek life or actively looking for a brotherhood, come chat with us, grab some info, and get connected. Come meet the guys. Learn about recruitment. events.title: Hands-Only CPR Demonstration - SLB Philanthropy Tabling Event
events.description: As part of Sigma Lambda Beta’s Founders Week, we have partnered with the Lincoln Fire & Rescue Department to host a stop-and-go Hands-Only CPR Demonstration open to anyone on campus. Sigma Lambda Beta’s national philanthropy focuses on CPR awareness and education, promoting the importance of learning this life-saving skill. At this tabling event, attendees can stop by to watch a brief demonstration and practice Hands-Only CPR using training dummies. This interactive opportunity allows anyone to quickly learn the basics of CPR and understand how immediate action during a cardiac emergency can help save lives.

events.title: Building the Blueprint: Navigating Unlikely Spaces and Finding Your Path Career Panel
events.description: Building the Blueprint: Navigating Unlikely Spaces and Finding Your Path is a panel hosted by the Freshman Action Team. Our mission this year is to bridge the gap between the classroom and the professional world by fostering representation where it is needed most. This professional panel will provide students with the opportunity to hear from leaders who have successfully navigated these spaces. Our representatives are going to share their authentic stories and potentially serve as a spark for a student’s future career. As Booker T. Washington famously said, "Success is to be measured not so much by the position that one has reached in life as by the obstacles which he has overcome." The event is on Thursday, April 2nd, from 4:00 PM to 6:00 PM. Prepare to stay for a short period afterward for networking. The location is in the Heritage Room in the Nebraska Union (1400 R St, Lincoln NE 68588).

events.title: College Republicans Weekly Meeting
events.description: Come hang out with the College Republicans! We are a registered group on campus dedicated to fostering and encouraging the activities of the Republican Party and promoting its ideals. We serve as an effective political voice through discussing and disseminating information on the local, state, national, and international levels. Our organization serves as a quasi-lobby in an effort to influence governmental affairs on a local, state, and national level. We encourage college students to express their political views through the philosophy of the Republican Party. Republicans believe in liberty, economic prosperity, preserving American values and traditions, and restoring the American dream for every citizen of this great nation. Through College Republicans, we wish to bring the next generation of Republicans into the party in order to grow and bring new ideas to the Republican Party through debate and discussion. We extend an invite to anyone on campus, regardless of their political orientation, background, or interest level to attend our weekly meetings to discuss the political issues going on in our local towns, cities, states, and nations, and have some fun!

events.description: This date’s activity is part of Life in Lincoln’s Global Kitchen cooking workshops, an interactive series that celebrates flavors from around the world. Cook alongside fellow Huskers, learn new skills, and enjoy a shared meal while exploring global cuisines and cultures. No prior cooking experience is needed. Join us for a fun, interactive cooking night inspired by easy, go-to meals from East and Southeast Asia. We’ll cook fried rice, glass noodles, quick kimchi, and fresh spring rolls – perfect for busy student life and shared kitchens. This workshop is all about learning practical skills, trying new flavors, and meeting new people. Everyone is welcome at the table!

events.description: Challenge Course Connections: Play, Problem-Solve, and Push Your Limits—Together Ready to meet other UNL students, be outside, and have some serious fun? At the UNL Challenge Course, you’ll jump into a series of engaging, ground‑based group activities designed to spark laughter, and stretch your problem‑solving muscles in a supportive, low‑pressure environment. Work together to navigate creative challenges, discover new ways to collaborate, and connect with people you might not have met otherwise. As your team gels, you’ll have the chance to push your personal comfort zone—and cheer others on—as the experience ramps up to opportunities on our 50' (15.24m) tall Alpine Tower. No experience needed—just bring your curiosity, your willingness to try something new, and your best “let’s‑do‑this” attitude. Come outside, meet your people, and build confidence one challenge at a time! Wear clothing appropriate for the weather and wear closed toed shoes. events.title: Shotokan Ohshima Karate-do & Self-Defense
events.description: Please see our website and Google calendar for the most up-to-date practice schedule: https://unl.ska.org/karate-class-schedule/ Shotokan Ohshima is a very traditional style and differs from many martial arts schools. Comprehensive, challenging exercise is only one of the many benefits of karate practice. Teaching traditional karate-do as a serious art and way of life means much more. It ensures the original ideas of the founder are passed on to our students, so that we grow in all aspects of our lives. Altman Senpai pushes us to challenge ourselves and taste our life, to be more fully aware and conscientious at all times. Our karate practice can be very difficult. The main difficulty lies in training one's mind to be disciplined and focused. Remember, anyone can be lazy, because that is the easiest thing to do! Karate may be defined as a weapon-less means of self-defense. These techniques use all parts of the body to their maximum advantage. Our fundamental techniques are divided into three main aspects: - Kihon — Fundamental blocks, punches, kicks, and stances- Kata — Pre-arranged forms simulating combat situations- Kumite — Sparring In each category, the beginner is given instruction at the most basic level until the techniques become spontaneous to them. As the student progresses technically, the practice progresses physically and the student begins to build greater stamina. At this stage, one involves himself or herself with the more intricate and difficult kata and more dynamic forms of kumite. Karate as self-defense is one of the most dynamic of all the martial arts. The trained karate student is able to coordinate the mind and body perfectly, thereby allowing one to unleash tremendous physical power at will. It is not the possession of great physical strength that makes a strong karateka; rather it is the ability to coordinate the mind and body. Upon developing this ability, even the smallest person finds that within themselves is the power to deliver a devastating blow to any would-be attacker. The values of karate to modern life are numerous. In our everyday lives we often forget the value of exercise to both our physical and mental health. The practice of karate tones the body, develops coordination, quickens reflexes, and builds stamina. Also, the serious practice of karate develops composure, a clearer thought process, deeper insight into one's mental capabilities, and more self-confidence. In this, karate is not an end, but a means to an end. It is an activity in which advancing age is not a hindrance. Rather, it encourages proficiency and the keen coordination of mind and body.

events.title: Lambda Chi Alpha and Alpha Omicron Pi Watermelon Bash
events.description: The Watermelon Bash is a collaborative philanthropic and social event hosted by Lambda Chi Alpha in partnership with Alpha Omicron Pi. The purpose of this event is to raise funds in support of Feeding America, a national nonprofit organization dedicated to fighting hunger and food insecurity across the United States. Funds will be raised through ticket sales. Tickets will be sold in advance for $5 when purchased from a member of Lambda Chi Alpha or Alpha Omicron Pi, and $7 at the door on the day of the event. Ticket sales will be managed by designated members of both organizations, with all funds collected, tracked, and deposited by the Lambda Chi Alpha executive board in accordance with chapter and university financial policies. The event consists of organized watermelon-themed games and activities, designed to create a fun, interactive, and inclusive environment for attendees. These games will be supervised by chapter leadership to ensure safety, structure, and compliance with university and organizational guidelines. All proceeds from the event will be donated directly to Feeding America to support hunger relief efforts and community food programs. The Watermelon Bash is intended to promote community engagement while raising both funds and awareness for an important philanthropic cause.

events.title: Erin Wohletz Artist Talk
events.description: Erin Wohletz is an Assistant Professor of Printmaking at the University of South Dakota. Originally from Las Vegas, Nevada they received their BFA in Printmaking and Painting from the University of Nevada, Reno and their MFA in Studio Art, Printmaking from the University of Tennessee, Knoxville. Erin Wohletz has participated in exhibitions across the United States including; Unfinished/ New Prints at the Print Center New York and Mid America Print Council Juried Members Exhibition at Kansas State University where they received Best in Show. Wohletz has participated in a variety of artist residencies including; Seacort Print Workshop in Bangor, Northern Ireland, Kala Art Institute in Berkley, California and the Morgan Paper Conservatory in Cleveland, Ohio. Erin also runs an online exhibition and index of LGBTQ+ printmakers called The Future is Queer.
